summary: Idea `idea-2835dace` created and promoted to the active research head.
reason: Use branch_alternative because the relation line should remain as a completed negative test, while the next idea should be a sibling route from the same earlier foundation rather than a child that keeps committing to teacher-side repair.

details.title: Query-Side Anti-Collapse Packet Repair After Relation Failure
details.problem: The relation-consistency repair became a second negative result on the same frozen widened bridge surface. Target packets remain perfectly discriminative, but both blueprint and relation repairs fail to recover predicted-to-target packet alignment, which suggests the remaining bottleneck is now more likely at the predicted packet or query-side head than in another nearby teacher-side auxiliary loss.
details.hypothesis: If the current bridge fails because query-side packet representations collapse before or during the predicted packet head, then a minimal query-side anti-collapse intervention or explicit query-head localization on the same frozen surface will recover more predicted-to-target discrimination than another teacher-side regularizer.
details.mechanism: Directly target the predicted packet or query-side head where discrimination is likely being lost, instead of adding another teacher-side relational auxiliary.
details.method_brief: Branch away from the failed relation repair line and return to the query-side bottleneck directly. Keep the same frozen widened bridge surface, packet exporter, and packet evaluator as controls. First expose or localize query-side packet-head / projection representations and then test one minimal anti-collapse repair, such as stop-gradient asymmetry, a variance floor, or a lightweight redundancy-reduction control, before re-evaluating on the exact same bounded packet interface.

selection_scores.why_winner: Relation adds a second negative result against teacher-side repair while the decisive evidence still points to lost predicted-side discrimination.
selection_scores.why_not_b: Another nearby teacher auxiliary would repeat a route already weakened by two measured failures.
selection_scores.why_not_c: Chunk-aware packaging alone no longer dominates because later widened evidence suggests the more direct bottleneck is query-side discrimination.

details.next_target: implement one bounded query-side localization-plus-repair package on the same frozen widened bridge surface and compare predicted-to-target packet metrics directly against both the downgraded blueprint run and the negative relation run.
risks: The bottleneck may lie earlier than the explicit query head, so a minimal anti-collapse control could improve variance diagnostics without restoring packet alignment.
The best next step may still turn out to be deeper interface restructuring rather than any local query-side repair.
Exposing extra query-side tensors may require modest exporter changes before the repair itself can be tested cleanly.

guidance_vm.summary: Idea branch is ready. Continue with the next active optimization stage on this durable research node.
guidance_vm.why_now: The accepted idea already has its durable branch/worktree, so the next leverage point is the configured next stage rather than another route-selection loop.
complete_when: The next configured stage starts from this branch.
The resulting evidence or decision is written durably.
